name: Whole Wheat Carrot-Pineapple Cake
recipeIngredient: 1 tsp cinnamon (or more to taste - I like LOTS)
1 tsp vanilla (I was out and didn't miss it)
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large mixing bowl, combine the dry ingredients: whole wheat flour, sugar, baking powder, baking soda, cinnamon, and salt.
2. Make a well in the center of the dry ingredients and add the remaining ingredients: applesauce, egg whites or Ener-G replacements, shredded carrots, canned pineapple juice, and vanilla.
3. Mix well using an electric mixer to achieve a light texture. Do not overmix.
4. Pour the batter into a Pam-sprayed 8 or 9 inch square pan.
5. Bake at 350°F (180°C) for 30-35 minutes, or until a toothpick inserted into the center comes out clean.
6. The cake is very moist and delicious without frosting. If desired, you can mix cream cheese, vanilla, and powdered sugar to create a simple frosting and spread it over the cooled cake.
